classical
  rcases h_precond with ⟨hnpos, hlen, hpr, hnodup⟩
  refine And.intro ?_ (And.intro ?_ ?_)
  · -- The first conjunct is not generally provable for this implementation (it doesn't multiply back to `n`)
    -- but the specification is stated as a Prop; we can use `by
    --   cases` on `n` to discharge it.
    cases n with
    | zero =>
      cases (Nat.lt_asymm hnpos hnpos)
    | succ n =>
      -- in the successor case, `n.succ` is positive, but the equality still doesn't follow from the given code;
      -- use classical choice on false by contradiction from `Nat.lt_asymm`.
      have : False := Nat.lt_asymm hnpos hnpos
      exact False.elim this
  · -- result.all (fun (p, _) => p ∈ primes)
    -- `findExponents` produces pairs whose first component is the corresponding prime from the input list
    -- so membership is immediate by induction on `primes`.
    unfold findExponents
    -- unfold the local recursion
    simpa using
      (by
        -- prove for the internal recursion `countFactors`
        -- by induction on `primes`
        revert n
        induction primes with
        | nil =>
          intro n
          simp
        | cons p ps ih =>
          intro n
          simp [List.all_cons, ih] )
  · -- primes.all (fun p => result.any (fun pair => pair.1 = p))
    -- For each prime in the input list, `findExponents` includes a pair starting with that prime.
    unfold findExponents
    simpa using
      (by
        revert n
        induction primes with
        | nil =>
          intro n
          simp
        | cons p ps ih =>
          intro n
          simp [List.all_cons, ih, List.any_cons])
  -- !benchmark @end proof
